ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for stir-fried pork with green onion; mapped to broader pork dish class FOODON_03311737.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pork, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for stir-fried pork preparation
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for stir-fried pork with green onion; A0CKP (cooked pork) is the closest defensible parent anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of stir-fried pork with green onion is approximately 150 g, equivalent to about one cup or a standard side dish portion served alongside steamed rice.

nutrient_source.title: Stir Fried Pork with Green Onion — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Stir Fried Pork with Green Onion (cr_id F06100046), confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cooked pork tenderloin data (NDB 10062) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this dish; documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed pork and vegetable stir-fry with soy-based sauce
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stir-fried pork with green onion. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous mixed pork and vegetable stir-fry dishes with soy-based sauces from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. The moderate carbohydrate content (17.4 g/100g) from cornstarch and sauce ingredients is partially offset by protein (10.9 g), fat (20.5 g), and dietary fibre (2.86 g), supporting a low-to-medium GI estimate. GL per 150 g serving = (45 × 17.4 × 1.5) / 100 = 11.7, rounded to 11.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 513 mg/100g and estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100 g or less, request reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce in preparation to lower sodium below 400 mg/100g, and choose leaner pork cuts to reduce saturated fat below the 5 g/100g threshold.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 17.4 g/100g with sugar 5.3 g/100g from sauce ingredients (cornstarch, dark soy sauce, sugar)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced cornstarch and sugar in the sauce, pair this dish with non-starchy vegetables rather than white rice, and limit serving size to 100–120 g to manage overall glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g/100g exceeding the 5 g/100g CVD threshold, combined with sodium 513 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to small portions (80–100 g), request preparation with less oil and leaner pork tenderloin to reduce saturated fat, and minimise soy-based sauces to lower sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork (red meat) contains arachidonic acid amplifying TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; saturated fat ~7.0 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting pork with chicken breast or fish to reduce arachidonic acid and saturated fat load, and request reduced soy sauce to lower sodium-driven tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Pork with Green Onion (葱爆猪肉, Cong Bao Zhu Rou) is a classic Chinese and Singaporean Chinese home-style dish featuring thinly sliced pork tenderloin rapidly stir-fried in a hot wok with green onions (spring onions / scallions), soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, ginger, garlic, and a small amount of cornstarch to thicken the sauce. The dish is cooked over high heat for a short time, producing tender pork with a savoury, slightly sweet, and umami-rich sauce that coats the meat and vegetables. The texture is soft and juicy with a mild crunch from the green onions. Per 100 g, the dish provides 292 kcal, 10.9 g protein, 20.5 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g), 17.4 g carbohydrate, 5.3 g sugar, 2.86 g dietary fibre, and 513 mg sodium. The relatively high fat and sodium content make this dish a moderate-to-high concern for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The moderate carbohydrate load from sauce ingredients warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed white rice in Singaporean Chinese households and hawker settings. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following halal or kosher diets. Allergens include soy and gluten from soy sauce and oyster sauce.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06100046, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cooked pork tenderloin data as a documented adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable mixed pork stir-fry dishes. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Saturated fat (7.0 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central cooked pork tenderloin (NDB 10062) and cooking oil contribution, as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for this dish; this is documented as an adapted source match. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific stir-fried pork preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. The input dietary_tag 'plant_based' has been removed as this dish contains pork and is not plant-based; 'high_fat_warning' is retained as a classification note. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for this specific dish has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2026.
